source_pr.title: *: avoid the generation of mysql.TypeNewDate
source_pr.body:
According to https://lists.mysql.com/internals/37677

`MYSQL_TYPE_NEWDATE` is an internal type of MySQL.
MySQL can handle both `MYSQL_TYPE_NEWDATE` and `MYSQL_TYPE_DATE`,
The only difference is that `MYSQL_TYPE_DATE` takes 4 bytes while
`MYSQL_TYPE_NEWDATE` takes 3 bytes to store. 

In TiDB, we only consider mysql.TypeDate,
so we should avoid the generation of mysql.TypeNewDate.

fix #5703 

PTAL @coocood @zz-jason

symptoms: r = tk.MustQuery("(select a from t where a < 0) union (select a from t where a > 0) order by a") (executor/executor_test.go)
failure_signature: r = tk.MustQuery("(select a from t where a < 0) union (select a from t where a > 0) order by a") (executor/executor_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR removes the internal MySQL `TypeNewDate` handling in TiDB (normalize to `TypeDate`) and updates a broad set of expression/types/server code and tests. The patch does not provide a concrete flaky failure signature or a targeted stabilization change for an intermittent failure, so we conservatively classify it as needs-more-evidence.
fix_pattern: N/A (insufficient evidence; no targeted stabilization change identified).

evidence: evidence: r = tk.MustQuery("(select a from t where a < 0) union (select a from t where a > 0) order by a") (executor/executor_test.go)

files: executor/executor_test.go, expression/builtin_cast.go, expression/builtin_compare_test.go, expression/builtin_time.go, expression/expr_to_pb_test.go, server/conn_stmt.go, server/util.go, table/column.go, types/convert_test.go, types/datum.go, types/etc.go, types/field_type.go, types/field_type_test.go
